recommended_sampling._comment: Four Qwen-official presets; use thinking_general as default for reasoning-capable use
recommended_sampling.notes: Default values are for thinking_general mode. Qwen publishes 4 presets (see nested objects). presence_penalty 0-2 reduces repetition; higher values may cause language-mixing. Small models (0.8B-9B) have thinking disabled by default — enable via chat_template_kwargs enable_thinking:true.

recommended_context.yarn_notes: YaRN RoPE scaling supported. To extend beyond 262,144 tokens set rope_type=yarn, factor=4.0, original_max_position_embeddings=262144 in config. Unsloth card recommends minimum 128K context to preserve thinking capability. llama.cpp: --rope-scaling yarn --rope-scale 4.0 for extended context.

chat_template.notes: Uses Qwen3.5 ChatML variant. Thinking generates <think>...</think> blocks before response. Small sizes (≤9B) have thinking OFF by default; pass enable_thinking:true to activate. llama.cpp: --chat-template-kwargs '{"enable_thinking":true}'

thinking.how: Generates <think>...</think> reasoning block before final answer. Enabled/disabled via chat_template_kwargs enable_thinking flag.
thinking.recommended: Enable for reasoning/complex tasks; disable (enable_thinking:false) for fast instruct/chat use. For llama.cpp: --chat-template-kwargs '{"enable_thinking":true}'. Use thinking_general preset (temp=1.0, top_p=0.95, top_k=20, presence_penalty=1.5) when thinking is active.

llama_cpp_notes: Load with: llama-server -hf unsloth/Qwen3.5-9B-GGUF:UD-Q4_K_XL. Recommended quant: Q4_K_XL (imatrix) or Q4_K_S minimum. Use --cache-type-k bf16 --cache-type-v bf16 if seeing gibberish output. For thinking: --chat-template-kwargs '{"enable_thinking":true}'. For YaRN at extended context: --rope-scaling yarn --rope-scale 4.0. BF16 KV cache helps stability. DavidAU GGUF minimum: Q4_KS (non-imatrix) or IQ3S (imatrix).

finetune_overrides.name: DavidAU/Qwen3.5-9B-Claude-4.6-OS-Auto-Variable-HERETIC-UNCENSORED-THINKING
finetune_overrides.description: DavidAU finetune trained on four Claude 4.6-OS datasets. Uncensored/heretic variant. KL divergence 0.0793 from base; refusal rate drops from 100/100 to 6/100.

davidau_specific.smoothing_note: Set smoothing_factor=1.5 in compatible UIs (KoboldCpp: Settings>Samplers>Advanced>Smooth_F; SillyTavern: Smoothing; text-gen-webui: Parameters lower right). Alternative: raise repetition_penalty to 1.1-1.15.
davidau_specific.repetition_penalty_range: 1.1 to 1.15 recommended as alternative to smoothing_factor

dry_sampler.note: DavidAU guide recommends DRY sampler for Class 3/4 models

finetune_overrides.notes: Sampling presets mirror base Qwen3.5 recommendations. DavidAU adds smoothing_factor=1.5 and rep_pen 1.1-1.15 as key differentiators. Auto-Variable refers to variable context/thinking integration via custom Jinja template. GGUF available at separate MAX-NEOCODE-Imatrix-GGUF repo.

gaps: 1) The specific 'OS' (Open Source) variant that matches local dir 'Qwen3.5-9B-Claude-4.6-OS-Auto-Variable-HERETIC-UNCENSORED' (non-THINKING, non-INSTRUCT suffix) was not found directly — closest THINKING and INSTRUCT variants were used; settings appear identical. 2) Auto-Variable technical definition not documented beyond 'custom Jinja + variable thinking'. 3) DavidAU smoothing_factor source verified from GGUF card but full sampler table may have more detail in Maximizing-Model-Performance README not fully parsed. 4) Qwen3.5-9B is multimodal (image-text-to-text) — vision inference params not researched.
